mnemonics.title: Capacity Planning Steps
mnemonics.content: Remember as D-CAP: Demand Analysis → Capacity Assessment → Action Planning → Performance Monitoring

mnemonics.title: Cloud Deployment Models
mnemonics.content: Remember as P-H-M-N: Private → Hybrid → Multi → Public (in increasing order of external dependency)

mnemonics.title: Scaling Types
mnemonics.content: Remember: UP = Vertical (add to existing), OUT = Horizontal (add more nodes)

acronyms.usage: Managing infrastructure through code definitions for automated deployment and scaling
Complete cost analysis including hardware, software, maintenance, energy, and personnel
Upfront investment in physical infrastructure and equipment
Ongoing operational costs for cloud services and maintenance
Cloud-based software delivery model affecting capacity planning decisions
Migration from physical vertical scaling to virtualized environments

visualTips.visualization: Picture a building: Vertical = adding more floors to existing building (taller); Horizontal = building more buildings in a complex (wider)
Think of a balloon: Private cloud is normal balloon size; peak demand makes it 'burst' into the public cloud (sky) for extra space
Virtualization = multiple houses on one land plot (each with own foundation); Containers = multiple apartments in one building (shared infrastructure, separate living spaces)
Think of a starfish: Central data center is the center, edge nodes are the arms processing locally near the source

analogies.analogy: Like upgrading a car engine from 4-cylinder to 8-cylinder - more power within the same vehicle frame
Like adding more checkout counters at a supermarket during busy hours - more parallel processing points
Like electricity: pay for what you use, no need to own a generator, scalable on demand
Like a recipe: instead of manually cooking each time, follow written instructions for consistent results
Like a modular Lego structure: each piece (service) can be replaced or scaled independently without rebuilding the entire model
